title: Squirrel Removal in New Brunswick, NJ | $900-$2,500
metaDescription: Professional squirrel removal services in New Brunswick, NJ. Humane trapping, exclusion & damage repair. Serving Rutgers campus & downtown areas. Call today for a free estimate!
intro: New Brunswick residents dealing with squirrels in attics, walls, or commercial properties need fast, effective removal services. Our squirrel removal experts serve the entire New Brunswick area, from the Rutgers University campus to historic downtown neighborhoods and the bustling Route 1 corridor. We provide humane trapping, permanent exclusion, and comprehensive attic restoration with pricing ranging from $900 to $2,500 depending on the severity of your infestation.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over 15 years of wildlife removal experience to New Brunswick, combining humane removal techniques with guaranteed exclusion work. Our technicians are fully licensed in New Jersey, familiar with local building codes and the unique challenges posed by New Brunswick's mix of historic homes, modern apartments, and university buildings.
localContext: New Brunswick's tree-lined streets near Buccleuch Park and mature neighborhoods around Rutgers create ideal habitats for squirrels seeking entry into attics and crawl spaces. The city's diverse architecture—from Victorian-era homes in the historic district to mid-rise apartments downtown—each presents unique access points that our experienced team knows how to identify and seal permanently.

faqs.question: How much does squirrel removal cost in New Brunswick, NJ?
faqs.answer: Squirrel removal in New Brunswick typically costs between $900 and $2,500, depending on factors like the number of squirrels, entry points, extent of attic damage, and whether repairs or sanitation are needed. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove squirrels from my New Brunswick home?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service throughout New Brunswick. The initial trapping process typically takes 3-7 days, followed by exclusion work to seal entry points. Complete removal and prevention usually takes 1-2 weeks depending on the complexity of your situation.

faqs.question: Do you service Rutgers University buildings and student housing?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide squirrel removal services for residential properties, commercial buildings, and multi-unit housing throughout New Brunswick, including areas near Rutgers campus. We work with property managers and landlords to address squirrel problems efficiently and minimize disruption to residents.

faqs.question: What damage can squirrels cause in New Brunswick attics?
faqs.answer: Squirrels can cause significant damage by chewing through electrical wiring (creating fire hazards), destroying insulation, gnawing on wooden beams and roof structures, and contaminating spaces with urine and feces. They're particularly active in fall and winter when seeking warm shelter, making early intervention crucial for New Brunswick homeowners.
